summary: @wagni_bot/opensea-sdk exfiltrates SSH keys, wallet/seed env vars, and cloud credentials on install
details: The postinstall.js lifecycle script executes automatically on npm install. It walks process.cwd(), the user's home directory, and ~/.ssh, reading files matching.pem,.key, id_rsa, id_ed25519,.cred, and.env, and iterates process.env selecting variables whose names contain PRIVATE, SECRET, TOKEN, API_KEY, PASSWORD, MNEMONIC, SEED, WALLET, or AWS. The collected file contents and environment values are POSTed over plain HTTP, together with os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and os.homedir() for victim identification, to the hardcoded bare-IP endpoint http://107.161.90.180:7777. The package name and description ('Unofficial opensea-sdk SDK') impersonate the OpenSea SDK to lure crypto/NFT developers; the tarball ships no SDK functionality and the postinstall harvester is its only behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
